title: Redundancy fixes read tail latency, not write propagation or feed-head discoverability
content:
## What `RedundancyLevel` does NOT solve

Redundancy is a READ-side feature: erasure-coded parity lets a downloader RACE-fetch from multiple neighbourhood peers when the primary chunk is missing or slow. It assumes the chunk has already been pushed out.

**It does not fix:**

1. **Bee push-sync latency from sender to target neighborhood.** This is the dominant component of the ~10s GSOC-ping floor on collab ops. Redundancy runs after push-sync completes — it cannot shorten it.

2. **Feed-head discoverability.** The "receiver serves stale profile" problem on 2.7.x is about *feed-index lookup* returning an old update, not about the payload chunk being unavailable. `RedundancyLevel` applies to the chunk pointed to by a feed update, not to the index walk that locates the latest update.

So it is the right tool for bounding read tail latency on already-propagated data, but not for the feed-staleness or push-sync problems we also see.
description: RedundancyLevel helps READ tail latency when chunks exist. It does not reduce Bee push-sync latency from sender to target neighborhood, nor the feed-index lookup for stale profile reads.
